replacement_detail_claim: The dome's V13 coordinate system introduces θ = −lonE as a 'unified angular coordinate rule,' replacing V12's solar-noon-UTC proxy. The dome claims this unifies both hemispheres with zero additional parameters.
replacement_detail_evidence:
WIN-058 claims that the angular coordinate in the dome frame is exactly θ = −lonE (negation of geographic east longitude), unifying Northern and Southern Hemisphere coordinate derivation. The dome presents this as a discovery: the V12 formula used solar noon UTC as a proxy for θ, producing catastrophic errors in the Southern Hemisphere (Sydney: +32.5° instead of the correct −151.2°, a 183° error). The V13 fix recognizes that solar noon UTC ≈ 12 − lonE/15h, so θ = (noonUTC − 12) × 15° = −lonE. The dome calls this a 'confirmed prediction.'

Kernel of truth: The dome correctly identifies a real bug in its own V12 coordinate system. The solar-noon-UTC proxy was mathematically equivalent to −lonE for Northern Hemisphere cities (where noon falls near 12:00 UTC) but broke catastrophically for Southern Hemisphere cities whose solar noon times produce angles in the wrong quadrant. Recognizing and fixing this is genuine model improvement — the V13 formula eliminates 180°+ errors in Southern Hemisphere cities.

Why this is not a prediction: The identity θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry. It is derived from the definition of longitude: geographic east longitude measures the angle east of Greenwich, and the dome's angular coordinate measures the angle west of Greenwich (since the dome's θ increases clockwise in the AE projection). The relationship θ = −lonE is a coordinate convention that follows from how longitude is defined — it is true on any map projection centered on the North Pole, not specifically on a flat disc. No dome-specific physics (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed) enters the derivation. The dome did not predict this relationship; it adopted the existing geographic convention it was already implicitly using.

The bug it fixes was self-inflicted: The 183° Sydney error existed only because V12 used solar noon UTC as an indirect proxy instead of longitude directly. Geographic east longitude has been available since the 18th century (following the adoption of the Greenwich meridian). The dome's V12 system introduced an unnecessary indirection (solar noon → θ) that garbled Southern Hemisphere angles. V13's 'discovery' that θ = −lonE is equivalent to recognizing that the proxy was unnecessary — the direct quantity was available all along. Correcting a self-inflicted bug is maintenance, not prediction.

WGS84 dependency persists: The θ = −lonE formula takes geographic east longitude as input. Geographic east longitude is defined by the WGS84 geodetic datum (or its predecessors). The dome's own OPEN-001 concedes: 'Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 — V12 uses longitude × 0.9941 as proxy.' V13 replaces the 0.9941 scaling with a direct longitude negation, but the WGS84 dependency remains — the dome still needs globe-derived coordinates as input. The dome's AI context page (V50.10) still lists 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' under OPEN-003, and the distance computation code (distance_analysis.py) still applies the 0.9941 factor: delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941. The V13 angular identity and the V12 scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.

Performance context: V13's θ = −lonE achieves 7.3% mean error for Northern Hemisphere same-hemisphere routes — a regression from V12's 5.2%. Cross-equatorial RMSE improved from 25–78% to 6.2%, but this improvement comes from the two-zone topology (WIN-057's rSH = 2 × 14,105 − rNH), not from the angular coordinate fix alone. Standard geodesy (Vincenty inverse formula on the WGS84 ellipsoid) achieves sub-0.01% accuracy globally with two parameters derived from rotational physics. The dome's coordinate system, even after the V13 fix, is 600–700× less accurate than the system it takes its inputs from. Near-equatorial cities remain excluded entirely: the dome's own OPEN-015 concedes Singapore (r = 23,556 km) 'falls beyond the equatorial ring' at req = 14,105 km.
replacement_detail_verdict_text: The identity θ = −lon<sub>E</sub> follows from the definition of geographic longitude, not from dome physics. Fixing a self-inflicted coordinate bug (the solar-noon-UTC proxy) is model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction. The dome's coordinate system still requires WGS84 longitude as input (OPEN-001 unresolved) and achieves 7.3% error where standard geodesy achieves sub-0.01%.
verdict_change_rationale: Changed from 'Not Demonstrated' to 'Misleading.' The curmudgeon review suggested Not Demonstrated, but upon examining the dome's actual V13 claim, 'Misleading' is more accurate. The dome presents a coordinate convention (θ = −lonE) that follows trivially from the definition of longitude as a 'confirmed prediction' of dome geometry. This is not merely undemonstrated — it actively misrepresents a bug fix as a discovery. The claim is circular (dome uses globe longitude to define dome angles) and misleading (bug fix framed as prediction).
replacement_finding: The identity θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic longitude, not dome physics; fixing V12's broken solar-noon proxy is maintenance, not prediction. WGS84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 700× worse than standard geodesy
holes_addressed: Critical: WIN-058 undefined on dome site — RESOLVED: dome has now defined it as θ = −lonE (V13, 2026-03-28)
Major: Our argument was 'coordinates are convention' — too abstract — RESOLVED: now argues the specific identity follows from longitude definition, not dome physics
Major: Torge & Müller citation misused — REMOVED: replaced with direct argument about longitude convention
Major: 0.9941 parameter origin not examined — RESOLVED: traced to distance_analysis.py, noted coexistence with V13
Moderate: Verdict category questioned — RESOLVED: changed to Misleading with explicit rationale
Moderate: WIN-057/058 conflation — RESOLVED: clearly separates angular fix (058) from two-zone topology (057)
new_evidence_added: Dome's actual V13 claim: θ = −lonE with Sydney 183° error correction
Code search: distance_analysis.py still uses 0.9941 factor alongside V13 θ = −lonE
Code search: generate_v13_warped_map.py confirms theta = math.radians(-lon_e)
OPEN-001 and OPEN-003 still reference WGS84 dependency and 0.9941 on context page
OPEN-015: Singapore r = 23,556 km exceeds r_eq, equatorial belt excluded
anticipated_objections: Objection: 'θ = −lonE is a genuine discovery — we didn't know it until V13.' Response: The identity follows from the definition of longitude. solar_noon_UTC ≈ 12 − lonE/15h is the same identity rearranged. The dome was already using −lonE implicitly in V12 (it worked for NH cities 'by accident' per the dome's own description). Recognizing the implicit relationship is debugging, not discovery.

Objection: 'Zero additional parameters makes it elegant.' Response: Zero additional parameters because it's not a new parameter — it's the removal of an unnecessary proxy. The dome went from an indirect measurement (solar noon UTC) to the direct quantity (longitude). This is simplification of a coordinate system, which is good engineering practice, but not a physical prediction.

Objection: 'It unifies both hemispheres.' Response: Any AE projection centered on the North Pole uses θ = −lonE (or equivalently θ = lonW). The unification comes from the map projection, not from dome physics.
kernel_of_truth: The dome correctly identified and fixed a serious bug in V12's coordinate system. The solar-noon-UTC proxy was a mathematically unstable proxy for longitude that failed catastrophically in the Southern Hemisphere. The fix (use longitude directly) improved cross-equatorial errors dramatically. This is genuine iterative model improvement — the dome author is working to make the coordinate system internally consistent. But bug-fixing is not the same as prediction, and the resulting system (7.3% NH error, equatorial cities excluded) still cannot compete with the globe geodesy it depends on for input.

key_findings.finding: delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941 — V12 scaling factor still used in distance computation. V13 θ = −lonE not implemented in distance code.
key_findings.significance: V13 angular identity and V12 distance formula coexist in codebase

key_findings.finding: theta = math.radians(-lon_e) in source_disc_point() — confirms θ = −lonE in V13 map generation code
key_findings.significance: V13 formula IS implemented for map rendering but NOT for distance computation

key_findings.finding: No mention of 0.9941, theta, lonE, angular, coordinate, or WIN-058
key_findings.significance: Core parameter file does not track coordinate system parameters

key_findings.finding: No coordinate validation. Uses Haversine (globe formula) for its own distance calculations: dlon_km = (lon2-lon1)*111.0*cos(lat)
key_findings.significance: Monitor.py uses globe geodesy internally while monitoring dome predictions

key_findings.finding: OPEN-001: 'Dome-native coordinates without WGS84' still open. OPEN-003: 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' still open.
key_findings.significance: Dome concedes WGS84 dependency and lack of physical basis for 0.9941, even as V13 claims to supersede it

code_search.v13_code_status: V13 θ = −lonE exists in map generation code only. Distance computation still uses V12 0.9941 scaling. No V13 two-zone distance calculator found in any repo file. The 6.2% RMSE claim on coordinates.html has no verifiable computation in the repository.
